  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Several public methods on the AuthenticationService that accepted a ConsentLevel parameter (e.g., HasPrimaryIdentity, GetPrimaryIdentity) have been removed. Embedders on iOS must update to the versions without this parameter as part of the ongoing sign-in refactoring. (c2453a6)
  • In-process Perfetto tracing is now enabled for developer profiling builds on iOS, improving performance analysis capabilities. (a02fdf9)

Sign-in and Identity

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public boolean field is_child_account has been removed from the GaiaAuthConsumer::ClientOAuthResult struct in google_apis/gaia/. Code that constructs or inspects this struct will break and should migrate to using Account Capabilities. (8165306)

Autofill

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The kCreditCardFull value has been removed from the public accessibility_annotator::EntryType enum. Code switching on or referencing this enum value will no longer compile and should be updated to handle the default kCreditCardNumber case. (83ac275)
  • A potential security bypass in the Autofill refill mechanism was fixed by preventing sensitive credit card fields from being filled without re-authentication. (426b928)

2026-04-22

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-22

Overview

This daily summary for the main branch on 2026-04-22 covers 649 commits, with 479 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from the first commit d80eb100687de7caeb714de06922e98b1949b68d to the last d8b766aca6cf560a969aa30424d6f1745b9acd65. This period includes a large number of breaking changes due to significant API and build system refactoring.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The long-standing BrowserList API (browser_list.h) has been completely removed. The public API surface of browser_finder.h was also reduced with the removal of FindBrowserWithID, FindBrowserWithWindow, FindBrowserWithActiveWindow, and FindBrowserWithUiElementContext. Embedders must migrate to newer mechanisms like BrowserWindowInterfaceIterator. (499cfae) (ba141b8) (af10f4e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A major build system refactoring moved browser and application lifetime headers (e.g., application_lifetime.h, browser_shutdown.h) from the monolithic //chrome/browser target to a new //chrome/browser/lifetime target. Embedders must explicitly add a dependency on the new target. (94c8534)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Web Feed feature has been completely removed. This deletes numerous C++ classes, Java bridges (WebFeedBridge), feature flags (kWebFeedAwareness), and public API methods like FeedActionDelegate.openWebFeed(). (c292b11) (1a47ae7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The pure virtual method GetURLLoaderFactory() has been removed from the public Profile interface and moved to its parent content::BrowserContext class. Code calling this method on a Profile object will no longer compile. (5436f47)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public Java methods have been removed from the Sync and Signin services on Android, including SyncService.isSyncFeatureEnabled(), SyncService.hasSyncConsent(), and SigninManager.revokeSyncConsent(). (f42b9fc) (0639353) (960d5db)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Autofill "Plus Address" feature has been removed. This includes the removal of GetPlusAddressDelegate() from the AutofillClient interface and related SuggestionType enum values. (ff574ae) (d65a964)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The default behavior of ContentBrowserClient::IsFullCookieAccessAllowed now returns false for storage keys created with a nonce, restricting cookie access in sandboxed or isolated contexts. This may affect embedder cookie handling. (7042a41)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for WebAppInstallationAcceptanceCallback has changed to support decoupling PWA installation from its launch. Callbacks now receive an additional closure to trigger the post-install launch action. (2a13b24)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for TabMenuModel now requires a TabStripModel* parameter, breaking existing instantiations. (37ca7f3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In Blink, the PathBuilder::CurrentPath() method now returns a Path by value instead of a const Path& to prevent use-after-free bugs. This is a public API signature change. (f3b97ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the default HTTP status code for navigations without response headers has changed from 200 to 0. This affects the NavigationHandleObserver interface and may impact error handling logic. (ca8ad98)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The JNI generation tooling no longer allows parameters for the @CalledByNative Java annotation. All instances of @CalledByNative("...") must be updated to @CalledByNative. (30d45a3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The google_toolbox_for_mac third-party dependency has been removed. Embedders that transitively depended on this library will experience build failures. (bcd5847)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API of AimEligibilityService was altered by removing the virtual method GetCountryCode(). Subclasses that override this method will no longer compile. (6ce8693)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public methods for showing "send tab to self" bubbles have been removed from the BrowserWindow interface. This functionality is now exposed through a dedicated BrowserWindowFeature. (ab08725)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The build_tflite_with_nnapi build flag has been removed. Embedders relying on this flag to enable the TFLite NNAPI delegate will have their builds broken. (6caa8b6)

Security

  • DevTools has been hardened by disallowing devtools:// access via Target.createTarget for untrusted clients and restricting file:// resource loading from remote frontends. A new --allow-unsafe-devtools-remote-file-loading flag is available as an escape hatch. (28c26ea) (8e5202f)
  • A Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability in DataObjectItem::GetAsFile was fixed by adding sequence number validation for clipboard items. (e59c8af)
  • A vulnerability where third-party SharedWorkers could bypass SameSite cookie restrictions when creating WebSocket connections has been fixed. (2b49183)
  • Security for Mojo IPC was hardened on Windows by improving remote process ID detection and adding an option to verify the server process's integrity level to prevent named pipe squatting attacks. (0e083d3)
  • A Use-After-Free in WebViewImpl::Minimize was fixed by using a weak pointer for a delayed task, and another in Page::OrdinaryPages() was fixed by copying the collection before iteration. (b1a2341) (7428099)
  • A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Cast was fixed by adding URL validation for media image URLs received from Cast devices. (16b9265)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The MergeFixedLayers and MergeStickyLayers rendering optimizations are now enabled by default to improve performance. (d80eb10)
  • The initiatorUrl for ResourceTiming is now enabled for dedicated and shared workers, providing more granular attribution for fetched resources. (dc78e7e)
  • A bug in AudioBufferSourceNode was fixed to correctly handle cases where start() is called without a source buffer attached, aligning with the Web Audio API specification. (ab5a5fd)
  • SVG filters using userSpaceOnUse will now correctly resolve percentage values against the viewport of the element where the filter is used, not where it was defined. (de5c226)
  • The <selectedcontent> element was updated to support <select multiple>, allowing it to clone and display content from all selected options. (3606758)
  • Video captions will now dynamically update when OS caption settings change on Windows, improving accessibility. (f593405)

Progressive Web Apps (PWA)

  • A graceful recovery flow was implemented for when the Web App Database is corrupted or fails to open. Instead of crashing, the browser will now attempt to salvage data, show a profile error dialog, and reset the PWA system. (6ffaab9)

Desktop & UI

  • The UI for the "Tabs from Other Devices" side panel has been implemented, showing a device picker and a list of tabs. (4f374b7)
  • Support for context menus on pinned action toolbar buttons was implemented as part of the WebUI toolbar migration. (1b3a82e)
  • A significant internal refactoring moved the WebContentsModalDialogManager delegate logic out of the Browser class and into a new BrowserWindowModalDialogDelegate. (083c2a8)
  • The chrome://policy page has been modernized by porting its promotion banner and export logic from legacy WebUI messages to Mojo. (e3cc2f5)

Android

  • The TranslatePrefs class was updated to store a list of recent target languages instead of a single one, in preparation for a richer language selection UI. (c691383)
  • Reload support was implemented for the native PDF page viewer, guarded by the InlinePdfV2 feature flag. (5dd34c7)
  • The media notification icon was changed from a sound icon to the Chrome icon to avoid user confusion. (9ab824f)
  • The list of supported media codecs for the MediaLauncherActivity was explicitly enumerated to prevent attempts to open unsupported file types. (17d2fdf)

iOS

  • Enterprise policy checks were implemented for passkey creation, ensuring compliance with PasswordManagerEnabled and other related policies. (dbab372)
  • Logic has been implemented to protect the Tab Grid from screenshots based on enterprise policies. (1ebf897)
  • The AuthenticationService API was updated to add new methods that do not require a ConsentLevel argument, as part of a larger cleanup effort. (e6b6aed)
  • A race condition was fixed in the Payment Methods UI that could cause unresponsiveness during rapid tapping. (24a6fcf)

Internals & Code Health

  • A new helper, base::WeakValueTable, was introduced for managing thread-safe shared caches of weakly-owned values. (dd21b7b)
  • A new public key encryption API (crypto::encrypt::Encrypt/Decrypt) was added to the crypto library. (34f8ea3)
  • A new helper function, ScaleByMemoryLimit, was introduced in base/memory_coordinator to provide a safe way to scale memory cache sizes based on system memory limits. (64428d6)
  • The media app accessibility code was extracted from the monolithic chrome/browser target into its own source_set to improve build times and dependency management. (cf9b018)
  • A large number of files were updated to migrate comments referencing old crbug.com issue IDs to the new issue tracker format. (abac920)

2026-04-21

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-21

Overview

This day saw 684 commits, with 496 deemed relevant to developers. The changes span from significant breaking API removals in the Remoting, Compositor, and Sync components to major feature cleanups like the removal of the WebNN DirectML backend and parts of the Web Feed feature. Key improvements include new capabilities for Android multi-window management, performance enhancements for the Omnibox, and security fixes for download resumption and renderer-initiated crashes. First commit: 054c47b1b245e62ccc13ccb3e6f9209eab288a35, last commit: 69a4fe10c99acd66f172d6770d5d5a25f35747d5.

API & Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy (non-WebRTC) remoting protocol has been significantly dismantled. Public classes BufferedSocketWriter, MessageReader, MessageDecoder, and the core VideoEncoder/VideoDecoder base classes have been removed from the public API. Embedders using the legacy Chromotocol protocol will be broken. (2b28768) (9cc0984)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core compositor class cc::OwnedLayerImplList has had several public, non-const accessor methods (e.g., begin, end, at) removed to enforce stricter API usage. Direct iteration or modification of the layer list by embedders is no longer possible and will require code changes. (c5db8df)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: In Blink's layout engine, individual margin accessors like MarginTop() and MarginLeft() have been removed from the public API of LayoutBox and LayoutBoxModelObject. Developers must now use the new MarginOutsets() method, which returns all margins in a single struct. (6cb32f8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public Java interface SyncService on Android has been cleaned up, removing the isSyncFeatureActive() and getSetupInProgressHandle() methods. Embedders must adapt to new ways of checking sync state. (6e99a22) (24cc34c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public GPU API class SharedImageInfo has been refactored to inherit from SharedImageMetadata. This changes how embedders access metadata fields (e.g., info.meta.size must be updated to info.size), requiring code updates. (722157f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Multiple public APIs had breaking signature or name changes. This includes FaviconHelper methods on Android adding a new parameter (ec7ce06e6d2a8f7011b9340696b013b8e39a74be), AccessibilityAnnotatorDatabase::DeleteContentAnnotation now taking a span (585ef24a33406f3b849c656ff81e6e82c4a81216), Android's PopupCreator::createPopupWindow being renamed (fadf008d299bb0a5e24c96bb3d5793dff82bbc22), and enum members in wtf::text::TextCodec::FlushBehavior being renamed (3b9e904f0510c9f89ba07eb2dae1b554fd8b05a4). (ec7ce06) (585ef24) (fadf008) (3b9e904)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A wide range of public APIs were removed. This includes: parsers from components/web_package (f64dbc334021a2cf51236f6061486996588a3f9e), ContextMenuDelegate::BuildMenuAsync() (378dd7bc488cc6f16850ed0abb8d6c08efb10933), the kLetAudioDescriptionFinish enum value from WebMediaPlayer::PauseReason (753802242b0872e8300c0871297fa912ee43c25f), methods from content::PrefetchContainer (4ec75a6c68768539df4d7c86795e40792b68a2ca), and an IPC from autofill_agent.mojom (2b232a587a7f0aed5f98def610333dc482a1b679). (f64dbc3) (378dd7b) (7538022) (4ec75a6) (2b232a5)

Feature Removals

  • The DirectML (DML) backend for the WebNN API has been completely removed. The ONNX Runtime (ORT) is now the sole supported path for DML on Windows. (ccff00c)
  • The 'Plus Addresses' feature is being deprecated. The settings entry point has been removed from the Android UI, and all associated PlusAddressMetrics have been deleted from the codebase. (089f13f) (1d3780c)
  • The ongoing removal of the Web Feed feature continues, with this batch removing the post-follow dialog and related metrics. (84bb07d)
  • The experimental Page Summary adaptive toolbar button, an AI-powered feature, has been removed along with its associated services. (f71a274)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The CSS image-animation property now correctly propagates from the root element to the viewport, fixing a bug where animations on the root's background would not play. (121e378)
  • Initial infrastructure has been added for the new CSS property text-decoration-skip-spaces. While the rendering logic is not yet implemented, the property is now parsed. (9e279b5)
  • Parsing for SVGStringList has been updated to correctly handle leading whitespace and empty items, improving compliance with HTML microsyntax specifications. (c73305f)

Android

  • Significant refactoring of multi-window and tab reparenting logic now allows for creating a new browser window from an existing WebContents. This improves flexibility for embedders managing multiple windows. (edfa5f2) (6e38ae5)
  • The BottomSheet component now supports a RESIZE_CONTENT height mode, enabling content to dynamically resize as the user drags the sheet. This was adopted by the Tab Bottom Sheet for a more fluid UX. (0c80f24) (6c2cc02)
  • WebView's cookie selection logic for intercepted requests was fixed to correctly respect Same-Site restrictions, preventing potential cross-site information leaks. (7b41029)

Security

  • A potential crash in VideoCaptureHost, caused by a compromised renderer calling ReleaseBuffer twice, is now handled gracefully by treating it as a 'bad message' instead of a CHECK failure. (6981dd9)
  • A security flaw in download resumption that could allow hash-based blocklist checks to be bypassed has been fixed. The hash state is now correctly reset when a download is resumed. (fc030cd)
  • When fetching a variations seed, X-Country and X-Geo-Level-1 headers are now only trusted over an HTTPS connection to prevent potential forgery via insecure networks. (536eb1d)
  • A Universal Cross-Site Scripting (UXSS) vulnerability in Reading Mode via the ttsEngine API was fixed by properly sanitizing language strings. (eb38552)

Desktop & Cross-Platform UI

  • A new TopChromeWebUIDialogDelegate has been introduced to simplify the creation of top-level WebUI dialogs, reducing boilerplate code. The default browser modal was refactored to use this new framework. (f14a8c0) (0e80a62)
  • To reduce latency on the critical path, database updates for the AutocompleteActionPredictor are now deferred to a low-priority background task. (5d63702)
  • On Wayland, window decorations will now correctly paint in an 'active' state during move and resize operations, improving visual consistency. This was achieved by separating the paint state from keyboard focus. (00dbd87)

Build System & Tooling

  • The /prefetch-inputs flag is now enabled by default for lld-link on Windows, which should improve linking performance, especially during cold cache builds. (8e295b0)
  • The Windows Template Library (WTL) dependency has been updated to version 10.1.0 and is now managed via CIPD, ensuring it stays up-to-date. (3a8ef69) (c08beab)
  • Efforts to improve build times and dependency hygiene continue with the modularization of several components, including //chrome/browser/new_tab_page, //chrome/browser/ssl, and //chrome/browser/metrics. (22a94a8) (124fb31) (2968416)

2026-04-20

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-20

Overview

This summary covers 440 relevant commits out of 601 total commits in the main branch on April 20, 2026. The changes span from the first commit 46394ace988541e7481f9446e9472fb3ea420dac to the last bbad371561e34913370ececf50bc29959838b7f7.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core & Networking: The return types of several public methods on net::URLRequest (e.g., GetTotalReceivedBytes) have been changed from int64_t to base::ByteSize, which will cause compilation errors for consumers. (8ade05b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core & Filesystem: The default behavior of base::DeletePathRecursively on Windows has changed to no longer traverse reparse points (like directory junctions). This can silently alter the behavior of file operations for embedders who relied on the previous traversal logic. (4ca74d8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Remoting: Legacy audio components have been removed from the Remoting API, including the public headers remoting/codec/audio_encoder.h and remoting/codec/audio_decoder.h. Embedders relying on this legacy audio pipeline will experience build failures. (df08c2f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING VIZ & Graphics: The signature of FrameSinkManagerImpl::SubmitHitTestRegionList has changed from void to bool. More importantly, the service will now disconnect clients that submit invalid hit-test data, which is a new failure mode for embedders. (b1585a0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Blink & Graphics: The stop member of the public struct blink::Gradient::ColorStop has been changed from a double to a float. This is a binary-incompatible change that will affect embedders who use this graphics utility directly. (2a25df3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android Sync: Several public methods have been removed from the components.sync.SyncService Java interface, including isInitialSyncFeatureSetupComplete(), hasKeepEverythingSynced(), and setSelectedTypes(). This will break embedders using the Sync-as-a-feature API. (d78abe4) (7fcfbca)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android Features: The Web Feed feature has been removed from Android, including its associated UI components (WebFeedMainMenuItem) and APIs. This is a removal of a stable, user-facing feature. (bafb125)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android Read Aloud: The public, though deprecated, methods getUrl() and getDescription() have been removed from PlaybackArgs.java in the Android Read Aloud module API. (52bdc97)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Autofill: The virtual method TriggerPlusAddressUserPerceptionSurvey has been removed from the public C++ interface autofill::AutofillClient. Embedders who implement this interface must remove their overrides. (2f6d1f8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING GuestView: The SlimWebView JavaScript API has been changed by replacing the allowedOrigins property with allowedOriginsParams. The internal SlimWebViewNavigationThrottle was also removed, breaking integrations that use this non-standard GuestView component. (10807a0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Privacy & Security: The public enum PrivacySandboxService::PromptSuppressedReason has been removed. Additionally, the public Mojo interface trust_tokens.mojom has been changed, removing the custom_key_commitment and custom_issuer fields. (41e4d73) (e4f0243)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Accessibility & FIDO: The signature of the public function ui::AXUrlBaseText has been changed to accept a std::u16string_view. Separately, the signature of device::enclave::Transact has been altered to return a std::unique_ptr to manage transaction lifecycle. (d1d0797) (c42180a)

Core, Internals & Performance

  • A large-scale refactoring was performed to cache the parsed GURL for the New Tab Page, avoiding repeated parsing across components like safe_browsing, sessions, and extensions to improve performance. (ec0f7b5) (9e92c17) (9abdf92) (910f0e9)
  • A use-after-free crash in SpdyStream was fixed by adding a WeakPtr check to prevent access after a session has started draining. (6810eeb)
  • The //third_party/afl fuzzer and the ink_stroke_modeler dependency have been removed from the build. The libc++ dependency was also rolled forward. (366e55c) (14b7b21) (1a580b5)
  • Checked Span memory safety checks have been enabled in release builds for canary and dev channels to help detect memory issues earlier. (41af5c6)

Blink, VIZ & Web Platform

  • Fonts: A new Mojo API, MatchLocalFont, was added to FontDataService to handle @font-face src: local() lookups in the browser process, with an initial implementation on Windows using DirectWrite. (662a4cc)
  • Layout: The implementation of LayoutBox client and content rect getters (e.g., ClientWidth) was refactored to use data from physical fragments instead of computed style, improving accuracy, especially in paged media. (a6f646c)
  • DOM: For performance, Bloom filters for static QualifiedNames (like HTML attributes) are now precomputed at initialization, speeding up attribute lookups. (8dec1ad)
  • WebAudio: A memory leak was fixed where AudioContext and AudioWorkletGlobalScope were not being reclaimed on AudioContext.close(). The fix involves explicitly terminating messaging proxies. (89899b8)
  • IndexedDB: Logic was added to handle cases where a renderer reports a blob size that differs from its actual size, preventing potential quota and data corruption issues. (5e6a8e5)
  • The enable-clipboardchange-event feature flag was removed, as the clipboardchange event is now a stable feature. (ec86ba4)

Security & Privacy

  • Network Security: A potential stack-based buffer overflow in the ONC (Open Network Configuration) message unpacker was fixed by adding validation for HMAC length. (fa1312b)
  • Safe Browsing: A request tier system was introduced to allow different types of client-side detection requests to block or bypass each other, improving resource management. (4ff2fbb)
  • Policies: A new policy, KioskPinchToZoomAllowed, was added to give administrators control over pinch-to-zoom in Kiosk sessions. (67c0264)
  • Privacy Sandbox: The PrivacySandboxPromptEnabled policy has been deprecated as the associated user prompt is being removed from the platform. (ff025fc)

Features & Services

  • Remoting: A large amount of legacy code related to the decommissioned "Chromotocol" protocol was removed, including P2PDatagramSocket, PseudoTCP components, and various channel factories. (59bb995) (76fc706) (053d46b)
  • Extensions: The unused webrtcAudioPrivate extension API was completely removed. Separately, the chrome.appviewTag API was restricted to ChromeOS only, as Chrome Apps are deprecated on other platforms. (6f72765) (a7c9dd8)
  • Autofill: A crash was fixed when merging Autofill profiles that contain an invalid phone number country code. (333c435)
  • Sync & Sign-in: A live browser test was added to verify Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) registration during the sign-in flow. (b306023)

Android & iOS

  • Android: A series of commits addressed Omnibox focus behavior related to the CompositorViewHolderObscuring feature, improving stability. (8ca40ce) (d77805d) (69be62c)
  • Android: A threading issue was fixed to ensure JniPaymentApp is always destroyed on the UI thread, preventing potential crashes in the payments flow. (1338451)
  • Android: The WebSerialWiredDevicesAndroid feature, which allows web pages to connect to wired serial devices, was enabled by default. (8cb21e2)
  • iOS: A new observer callback, FullscreenDidTransition, was added to notify when fullscreen transitions are fully complete. (fde9a76)
  • iOS: Scroll position restoration for "Send Tab to Self" notifications was implemented, improving the user experience when opening shared tabs. (7252cda)

2026-04-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-19

Overview

This digest covers 29 commits, highlighting two major breaking changes in UI components and Chrome Remote Desktop. Other key developments include significant internal refactoring of the networking stack's disk cache, numerous test enhancements and bug fixes for the HTML canvas element, and ongoing build system modularization.

UI & Views

  • ⚠️ BREAKING A refactor of tab hover cards introduces a breaking change by replacing the HoverCardAnchorTarget::GetAnchorView() virtual method with a new pure virtual method, GetAnchor(), which returns a views::BubbleAnchor instead of a views::View*. Embedders or custom UI components subclassing HoverCardAnchorTarget must update their implementation to provide the new anchor type. (174be1f)
  • Various UI enhancements were made, including persisting the window size for auto Picture-in-Picture on Android, reordering buttons on the profile creation screen for better readability, and temporarily disabling animations on the first-run sign-in page. (b5af6f8) (e73f207) (88eebcc)

Chrome Remote Desktop

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The host setup logic on Linux has been updated, introducing a breaking change to the remoting::StartHostAsRoot function. Its signature has been changed from int StartHostAsRoot(int argc, char** argv) to int StartHostAsRoot(), removing its parameters. Scripts or tools that call this function directly will need to be updated. (24cd9c6)

Networking

  • The SQL-based disk cache backend received significant internal updates to improve asynchronous task management. New classes like SqlAsyncTaskManager and SqlTrackedSequenceBound were introduced to provide more reliable synchronization for testing purposes, ensuring that complex, multi-step operations can be tracked to completion without relying on RunUntilIdle(). (0e4d1c9) (d809804) (38c4f09)

Blink & Web Platform

  • Extensive work was done to improve the robustness of the 2D canvas implementation. This includes adding comprehensive web tests for context loss and restoration scenarios, especially when combined with GPU process loss or invalid canvas sizes. A bug was also fixed where a resource dispatcher was not correctly recreated after a context was restored, affecting desynchronized canvases. (9b65e50) (676e2fd) (70940fb) (cc55330) (3c95ea3) (0ccd8f8) (ff41cf0)
  • Regular Web Platform Test (WPT) imports bring in new tests and expectations. (17970ac) (91342bb) (a942ac1)

Autofill & Payments

  • Autofill sees several updates, including new metrics to differentiate between suggestions for cards saved externally (e.g., via Google Play) versus newly-synced cards. On iOS, the logic for displaying passport details was improved. The in-product help bubble for saved cards was also updated to mention 'Google Wallet' for brand consistency. (6fb6cb0) (d481fcf) (7ba1c54)

Build & Infrastructure

  • Ongoing modularization efforts continue, with //chrome/browser/support_tool and //chrome/browser/ntp_customization being extracted into their own source sets. The build dependency script install-build-deps.py was updated for compatibility with Debian 'sid'. (0f60250) (ba57552) (5698a61)
  • Several expired histograms were removed from the codebase, and CI was updated to run gtests on betty for a percentage of Googler CLs. (3db3a99) (2cbe2ff) (2a3b985)

Security

  • A security vulnerability was addressed in the VA-API JPEG encoder (VaapiJpegEncodeAccelerator) by adding an explicit bounds check to prevent a potential out-of-bounds write during the encoding process. (c24fe50)

2026-04-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-18

Overview

This digest covers 48 commits, highlighting a significant breaking change in the core url library. Key themes include the ongoing removal of legacy code in the Remoting component, major refactoring to eliminate ATL dependencies on Windows, and the shipment of new web platform features like CSSPseudoElement. Other changes involve accessibility improvements, bug fixes, and routine test maintenance.

Core

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Removes the deprecated IsStandard(const char* spec, const Component& scheme) overload from the public url library API. This function was marked for removal and is now gone. Embedders relying on this unsafe, char*-based function must migrate to the IsStandard(std::optional<std::string_view> scheme) overload to avoid build failures. (83cace3)

Internals & Refactoring

  • Removes legacy ICE (Interactive Connectivity Establishment) components from the Remoting stack, including IceTransport, IceTransportChannel, IceConnectionToClient, and IceConnectionToHost. This is part of a larger effort to modernize the connection protocol and remove the old "Chromotocol". (e8ee844) (14c775e)
  • Continues the large-scale effort to remove dependencies on the Windows-specific Active Template Library (ATL). This change replaces ATL security classes like CSecurityDesc and CSid with cross-platform base::win equivalents in the updater and enterprise companion components. (c7f06b9)
  • Enhances WTF::String by adding new Split() and SplitSkippingEmpty() overloads that accept a function predicate. This provides a more flexible and powerful mechanism for string splitting, replacing older, less generic implementations. (610a506)
  • Cleans up obsolete code by removing the unused manifest_update_utils files for Progressive Web Apps (PWAs), as the logic has been migrated to a modern job-based architecture. (9338d92)

Web Platform

  • Enables the CSSPseudoElement interface and the event.pseudoTarget property by default. This change ships new web-facing APIs that allow developers to interact with pseudo-elements in JavaScript. (583bc39)

Accessibility (a11y)

  • Fixes an issue on macOS where VoiceOver would incorrectly announce edits to <input type="number"> (spin button) fields. The change prevents text edit notifications for value changes made via stepping, ensuring the screen reader announces the full new value correctly. (9bfcec8)

Android

  • Corrects camera frame rotation logic for external cameras on Android. The feed from an external camera will no longer rotate with the device, fixing incorrect video orientation. (182b818)

2026-04-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-17

Overview

A busy day with 763 total commits on the main branch, of which 546 were deemed relevant for this summary. The changes span from major API removals to web platform advancements and significant security hardening. The first commit of the day was (d5044d6) and the last was (9a36092).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy synchronous OSCrypt backend (components/os_crypt/sync) has been completely removed. Embedders must migrate from the public OSCrypt::EncryptString and OSCrypt::DecryptString APIs to the os_crypt/async backend. (a4cab4a) (21b80f4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new virtual method, OnFedCmFederatedLogin, has been added to the public content::WebContentsObserver interface. Embedders with custom observer implementations must add this method to compile. (17d438b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public ui::OzonePlatform interface was refactored, renaming PreEarlyInitialization() to PreSandboxStartup(). This will break custom Ozone platform implementations. (970a6d5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Java interface method SwipeGestureListener.SwipeHandler#isSwipeEnabled() now requires a MotionEvent parameter, breaking all existing implementations. (86a8f48)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The behavior of the stable CSS :lang() pseudo-class has been changed to no longer match elements with ill-formed lang attributes. This aligns with the CSSWG specification but may break the styling of websites that relied on the previous, more lenient behavior. (95aa378)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The navigator.credentials.get() web API will now throw a NotSupportedError when called without a credential type, aligning with the specification. This replaces the previous behavior of returning null and may break websites that relied on it. (b17a514)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple public C++ APIs were removed or changed, requiring code updates for embedders. This includes the removal of net::HashValue::DeprecatedFromString, passage_embeddings::Embedding::Dimensions(), and ToolbarButtonProvider::GetAvatarToolbarButton(), and signature changes to PrePrefetchService::Create and MediaWebContentsObserver::OnMediaMetadataChanged. (104e45e) (49a6256) (6c9ccc1) (7a24f21) (1a7e135)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public Java interfaces on Android were changed, breaking embedder implementations. This includes the removal of openInAppOrChromeFromCct from TabWebContentsDelegateAndroid and the renaming of the PopupIntentCreator interface to PopupCreator. (05bf5a2) (690a2ae) (a6db4e8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public mojo interface blink::mojom::DevicePostureProvider was changed to remove emulation methods, and the return type of RenderWidgetHostDelegate::GetDevicePostureProvider was also changed, breaking embedder implementations. (829bf4e)

Security

  • Sandbox and IPC hardening continues. Browser-side sandbox enforcement was added for kPointerLock, and the extensions EventRouter now performs stricter validation on listener registration to prevent renderer exploits. (80ddc28) (5cca17e)
  • A potential Origin header spoofing vulnerability during cross-origin redirects was mitigated by rejecting requests where the Origin header is modified. (fd71efb)
  • On Windows, Module Tampering Protection is now enabled for Code Integrity Guard (CIG) processes to prevent unauthorized DLL injection. (60632ed)
  • A SameSite Strict cookie bypass was fixed in Android's "Preview page" feature by correctly passing the initiator origin. (62735f6)
  • On macOS, depth limits were added to bookmark pasteboard helpers to prevent stack overflow crashes from maliciously crafted clipboard data. (c5f67dc)
  • A timeout was added to the Variations Service seed fetch request to mitigate a potential attack vector that could bypass certificate expiration checks. (ad917ff)

Web Platform & Blink

  • A new performance.speculations.preloads API was added, allowing web developers to access measurement data for preloaded resources. (2f2079d)
  • HTML parser performance was significantly improved for pages with large inline scripts or styles by throttling unnecessary flushes, which previously caused O(n^2) behavior. (1e0d769)
  • The compositor will now merge pending layers for sticky and fixed position elements with compatible constraints, reducing layer count and improving performance. (dc6a5be) (73b7cd8)
  • Support for parsing declarative shadow roots in stream*HTML was implemented. (b782d6c)
  • A console warning will now be emitted when a programmatic focus call is denied by the focus-without-user-activation permissions policy, improving developer experience. (b0e008f)
  • A significant bug was fixed where click events were dropped on ephemeral pseudo-elements (e.g., :active::after) that were destroyed during event dispatch. (82777e8)

Features, UI & UX

  • The Android "Creator" page, part of the Web Feed feature, has been removed. This includes the CreatorActivity and related logic. (5653d2b)
  • The setting to show memory usage on tab hover cards has been deprecated and removed from the UI. (7df2960)
  • A new precedence-ordered registry for MIME handlers was added for extensions, allowing for more predictable handler selection. (acf2d3c)
  • On Linux, a new NativeFrameViewLinux class was introduced to provide native client-side decorations for non-browser widgets when using the GTK UI theme, improving the appearance of dialogs. (e0f759f)
  • A crash when restoring tab groups has been fixed. The issue occurred when closing a window with multiple groups and then restoring them into different windows. (e8ab45b)

Platform Integration

  • On Windows, a new Gamepad data fetcher based on the modern GameInput API was implemented, improving support for modern controllers. (ce05dce)
  • On Android, a use-after-free crash in ModalDialogWrapper was fixed. The crash occurred if a dialog was dismissed by the OS after its native counterpart was destroyed. (8f0f462)
  • On Android, a fix was implemented in WindowAndroid to correctly update its associated display when an activity is not recreated on display change, preventing incorrect UI scaling. (766e18e)
  • For enterprise reporting, a new StartupCommandLineCollector class was added to capture command-line switches used at browser startup. (380c663)
  • The Target.TargetInfo object in the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) now exposes a parentId, providing more context for targets like dedicated workers. (96647e3)

Build & Code Health

  • A significant effort to modularize //chrome/browser continues. Multiple components, including notifications, subresource_filter, tab_group_suggestion, resource_coordinator, first_run, policy, and local_discovery, were moved out of the monolithic :browser target. (4ae8b1e) (e514a41) (2fc215c) (8ca1b4c) (0370215) (70f09b8) (1b7e251)
  • Key development dependencies were updated, including Xcode to version 17E202 and ESLint to 10.2.0. (287de9b) (df9f4e9)
  • The build system was updated to support Ubuntu 26.04. (c9042f7)
  • PaintOp serialization and deserialization APIs were modernized to use base::span instead of raw pointers, improving memory safety. (283a033)
  • An obsolete and unused file (mapped_file_bypass_mmap_posix.cc), which was a 13-year-old hack for early Android devices, was removed from the codebase. (e4763b0)

2026-04-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-16

Overview

This summary covers 705 commits on the main branch from (681ab58)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((681ab58)) to (e9645d9)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((e9645d9)), with 539 commits identified as relevant to developers. The day saw a high number of breaking changes across core components, including the password manager, rendering APIs, JNI wrappers, and ChromeOS enrollment behavior. Major refactoring efforts continue in the password manager and browser component modularization.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Password Manager: A major refactoring has begun to replace the PasswordForm struct with StoredCredential. This changes the public API of LoginDatabase, affecting methods like AddLogin and UpdateLogin, and will require significant updates for embedders interacting with the password store. (fa3099d) (167b1b4) (a4434fb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING ChromeOS: The auto-enrollment process will now trigger a mandatory device wipe if it detects the device is already owned. This is a security hardening measure but represents a significant behavioral change for enterprise device setup workflows. (a602cec)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android JNI: The ByteArrayView helper class has been removed from the public JNI wrappers (jni_wrappers.h). Downstream code must be updated to use JArrayView<int8_t> instead. (1e57046)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Rendering & Animation: The public animation.mojom interface used by the Viz compositor has changed. time_offset has been replaced with hold_time in KeyframeModel to align with the Web Animations API (WAAPI). (bc70524)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Browser Utilities: The return type of the core utility function chrome::FindLastActiveWithProfile has been changed from Browser* to BrowserWindowInterface*. Callers must be updated to handle the new interface type. (e8d0df4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Tracing: A new pure virtual method, CreateChromeMetadataPacketRecorder(), has been added to the content::TracingDelegate interface. Embedders who provide a custom implementation of this delegate must add an implementation for the new method. (853791d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Media: The legacy IPC ParamTraits for media::AudioParameters and media::ChannelLayout have been removed in favor of modern Mojo traits. Embedders relying on the old IPC mechanism for these types will break. (d8a0c10)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extensions: The permissions.addHostAccessRequest and permissions.removeHostAccessRequest APIs now require a user gesture, preventing programmatic invocation without user interaction. This is a behavioral breaking change to mitigate clickjacking. (1aca864)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Web Apps: The WebApp::last_launch_time() method signature has changed from returning const base::Time& to const std::optional<base::Time>&, requiring consumer code updates. (66b19f5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Rendering: The public virtual method StyleImage::IsAccessAllowed() has been renamed to IsCorsSameOrigin(). Subclasses or callers must be updated to use the new name. (4bffaa6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android UI: The factory for creating JavaScript dialogs, AppModalViewFactory, now accepts a std::unique_ptr<AppModalDialogController> instead of a raw pointer, breaking custom implementations. (457861e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Autofill: The autofill::PasswordSuggestionRequest struct has been modified; several fields have changed type from FieldRendererId to FieldGlobalId. Code constructing this object must be updated. (9e5d4e8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Feature Removal: The legacy text autosizer feature has been completely removed from the rendering engine. (f8c89b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android UI: The AutofillSaveCardInfoBar class and its related files have been removed, as the UI has been replaced by modern message and bottom sheet implementations. (27700ee)

Blink, Rendering, and CSS

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public animation.mojom interface used by the Viz compositor has changed. time_offset has been replaced with hold_time in KeyframeModel to align with the Web Animations API (WAAPI). (bc70524)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public virtual method StyleImage::IsAccessAllowed() has been renamed to IsCorsSameOrigin() to better reflect its purpose. Subclasses or callers must be updated. (4bffaa6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy text autosizer feature and its related web tests have been removed from the rendering engine. (f8c89b4)
  • CSS: The AccentColor and AccentColorText CSS keywords are now enabled by default, allowing web content to use the system accent color. (00200ea)
  • CSS: The experimental balance <integer> syntax for flex-wrap has been removed. It is being replaced by a new flex-line-count property, controlled by the FlexWrapBalance feature flag. (169e28c)
  • The focus-without-user-activation permissions policy has been refined to allow a frame to manage focus within its own descendants, preventing focus theft while enabling legitimate use cases like delegating focus to child iframes. (362fb0b)
  • A bug was fixed where overflow:hidden would fail to clip child elements positioned over transparent borders. (530bc72)
  • The viz::AggregatedFrame struct was updated to include TrackedElementRects, which aggregates tracked element bounds from all surfaces into the root coordinate space. (cc6fcac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The unused hotword_enabled field has been removed from the public blink::StreamControls struct and its corresponding mojom interface. (2c49460)

Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: To mitigate clickjacking, the permissions.addHostAccessRequest and permissions.removeHostAccessRequest extension APIs now require a user gesture. (1aca864)
  • A path traversal vulnerability in drag-and-drop operations has been fixed by sanitizing the filename extension provided by the renderer. (5ef4980)
  • A potential sandbox escape on ChromeOS was fixed by adding validation for Bluetooth MAC addresses in the brailleDisplayPrivate extension API. (4504ead)
  • For HLS playback, a fix was implemented to reject tainted, redirected encryption keys. This adds a new non-pure virtual method DidRedirect() to the media::DataSource public interface. (a8db6f5)
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in RootView::HandleMouseEnteredOrMoved was fixed by using a ViewTracker to safely handle views that are deleted during event dispatch. (dd17b61)
  • The ContentWalletablePassIngestionController was updated to use the trusted URL from the RenderFrameHost instead of a renderer-provided URL, fixing a security issue. (f842c3a)

Android

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The ByteArrayView utility has been removed from the public JNI wrappers. Downstream embedders must update their native code to use JArrayView<int8_t>. (1e57046)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The AutofillSaveCardInfoBar class and its associated native and Java files have been removed, as this UI has been replaced by modern systems. (27700ee)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The factory for creating JavaScript dialogs, AppModalViewFactory, now uses std::unique_ptr for the controller, breaking custom implementations. (457861e)
  • The FluidResize feature for tablets and desktop devices has been enabled by default. (97da232)
  • Link Capturing on desktop Android was enabled by default. (30a7542)
  • The Android Handoff API has been integrated to allow for seamless session transfer between a user's devices. (a2f9ed1)
  • A new keyboard shortcut (Alt+Home) was added to open the home page. (2d6da44)
  • To comply with new OS requirements, the REQUEST_FULLSCREEN_MODE permission has been added to the manifest. (a17e747)

Autofill and Passwords

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A major refactoring has begun to replace PasswordForm with StoredCredential. This changes the public API of LoginDatabase and other password store components. (fa3099d) (167b1b4) (a4434fb) (6b01163)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The autofill::PasswordSuggestionRequest struct has been modified; fields username_field_id and password_field_id were changed from type FieldRendererId to FieldGlobalId. (9e5d4e8)
  • The autofillPrivate API was updated to include an isReadOnly field for entity instances, supporting read-only data from external sources. (86a2bbe)
  • A new validation rule was added to Autofill to discard forms where <select> elements have a non-zero max_length, improving data integrity. (c6ff94f)

Extensions and Web Apps

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The WebApp::last_launch_time() method's signature was changed from const base::Time& to const std::optional<base::Time>&. (66b19f5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for IsolatedWebAppValidator::ValidateIntegrityBlockAndMetadata was changed to enforce stricter key rotation rules, breaking existing callers. (096fc41)
  • The requirement for --remote-debugging-pipe and --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging flags for debugging extensions via CDP has been removed, simplifying the developer workflow. (dddf05b)
  • The feature flag enable-printing-margins-and-scale was removed, making the ability for extensions to control printing margins and scale permanently enabled. (035abaf)
  • A large refactoring of the PDF viewer's MimeHandlerStreamManager introduces a new MimeHandlerStreamDelegate interface to separate generic stream management from PDF-specific logic. (85e6b90) (197a2fb)

Media and Audio

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The serialization mechanism for media::AudioParameters and media::ChannelLayout has been migrated from legacy IPC ParamTraits to modern Mojo traits, breaking embedders using the old system. (d8a0c10)
  • A new natural_size field has been added to the VideoFrameInfo mojom interface, allowing producers to specify a display size different from the frame's visible rectangle. (0cbd129)
  • A race condition in MediaDrmBridge on Android was fixed by adding synchronization to ensure destroy() waits for in-flight JNI callbacks to complete. (5625d2e)
  • The third-party libaom AV1 codec library was updated, bringing in fixes for thread safety and heap reads. (bd46ccd)

Developer Tools and Tracing

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The content::TracingDelegate interface has a new pure virtual method, CreateChromeMetadataPacketRecorder(), which must be implemented by all subclasses. (853791d)
  • Frequent but low-signal trace events in the navigation throttle have been disabled by default to reduce tracing overhead during performance analysis. (23d6bdc)
  • A memory corruption bug in DevTools' NetworkResourcesData class was fixed by ensuring resource IDs are correctly managed during cleanup. (4e48524)

Build System and Refactoring

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The return type of the core utility function chrome::FindLastActiveWithProfile has been changed from Browser* to BrowserWindowInterface* as part of the "Bedrock" refactoring project. (e8d0df4)
  • Ongoing modularization efforts continue to reduce dependencies on the top-level //chrome/browser target. Components moved or componentized include //chrome/browser/privacy_sandbox, //chrome/browser/share, and parts of //extensions. (92b2a6c) (3ef188b) (c1c2d43)
  • The password manager component was modularized into separate public (//components/password_manager/public/core) and implementation targets to improve the build graph. (9ddae42)

2026-04-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-15

Overview

This digest covers 712 commits on 2026-04-15, with 549 deemed relevant to developers. The day's changes span from (b572266)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((b572266)) to (170c470)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((170c470)). This was a significant day for breaking changes, including the removal of several Chrome App APIs on desktop, a major overhaul of networking Mojo interfaces, and security-driven behavioral changes to web workers and media requests. Developers on Chromium-based projects should pay close attention to the API and behavioral changes listed below.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.usb, chrome.bluetooth, chrome.bluetoothLowEnergy, chrome.bluetoothSocket, and chrome.bluetoothPrivate APIs have been removed from Windows, Mac, and Linux builds. These APIs are now restricted to ChromeOS only, breaking Chrome Apps that rely on this functionality on desktop platforms. (32b3c6d) (1e44322)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core networking Mojo interfaces in network_param.mojom have been overhauled. Opaque [Native] types for HttpResponseHeaders, SSLInfo, and CertVerifyResult are replaced with fully structured Mojo types, requiring embedders to update their code to handle the new data structures. (d825538)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The default behavior of data: URL Web Workers has changed to give them unique, opaque origins, aligning with web standards. This may break applications that relied on workers inheriting the creator's origin. An enterprise policy, DataUrlInWebWorkerOpaqueOriginEnabled, is available as a temporary escape hatch. (8d1a1e1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING For security reasons, cross-origin media requests that use byte ranges (HTTP Range Requests) are now disallowed. This will primarily break HLS (HTTP Live Streaming) playback scenarios that rely on this pattern. (d2e04a1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the default behavior of base::ObserverList now disallows re-entrant notifications. Code that modifies an observer list while it is being iterated over may now crash or experience undefined behavior. (a0109f9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public methods related to the bookmark bar, including IsBookmarkBarVisible() and FocusBookmarksToolbar(), have been removed from the core BrowserWindow interface. This functionality is now delegated to BookmarkBarController. (aaed67f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of the public mojo interface TabStripService::Update has been changed to include a new update_mask parameter to support partial updates. Clients of this service must update their calls. (b54c1e9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of the public WebID/FedCM API method content::IdentityRequestDialogController::ShowModalDialog has been changed to include a new ShownModalAsyncCallback parameter. (47ad706)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public JNI helper type alias base::android::JavaObjectArrayReader has been removed. Code should be updated to use the iterable JArrayView instead. (40e32d7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public methods ClientWidthFrom and ClientHeightFrom have been removed from the core Blink class LayoutBox following the removal of the text autosizer feature. (2c0162e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The net::HashValue::FromString method has been refactored. The non-static method is renamed to DeprecatedFromString, and a new static factory method FromString that returns an std::optional<HashValue> is introduced. (11fbf92)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The "Contextual Tasks" native page feature on Android has been removed entirely, including the ContextualTasksNativePage class, NativePageType.CONTEXTUAL_TASKS enum, and associated URL constants. (35007fa)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Internal constants for the Viz compositor frame lifecycle have been changed. kFrameIndexStart is now 1 (was 2) and kLocalFrameToken has been removed, which may impact direct integrations with the Viz service. (43d9240)

Security & Privacy

  • Multiple Use-After-Free (UAF) and memory safety vulnerabilities were addressed in components including Cast streaming, fullscreen exit navigation, FedCM UI, FileSystemAccessBucketPathWatcher, and TabStripModel. (49ef28b) (8e95aab) (60dc402) (cfafd42) (9250628)
  • A Mojo deserialization vulnerability was patched by adding mode validation for shared memory regions, preventing a compromised process from crashing the browser. (ac68339)
  • The payment manifest cache key now uses the full URL spec instead of a hash-based string to prevent a potential hash collision attack. This change will invalidate the old cache once. (cf568b2)
  • The DeveloperToolsAvailability policy was hardened to prevent several bypasses, ensuring that inspection is correctly disallowed for force-installed extensions. (e5f8ed7) (bfdcab0)
  • A potential Out-of-Memory (OOM) crash in WebAudio's decodeAudioData was mitigated by gracefully handling allocation failures. (f59bc9f)

Web Platform & Blink

  • The CSS shape-outside property was extended to support path() and shape() functions, gated by the ShapeFunctions feature flag. (14981db)
  • Custom Elements with submit behaviors are now recognized for implicit form submission. This also enables the :default pseudo-class and the dialog submission method for custom elements. (c46fc55)
  • The new behavior for user-action pseudo-classes (e.g., :active) interacting with top-layer elements like <dialog> is now enabled by default. (25dd424)
  • The legacy default border-color:gray rule for <table> elements has been removed and shipped, aligning with web standards. (44c0b07)
  • SVG's animateMotion now correctly respects the CSS d property, not just the d attribute, when defining an animation path. (e9cd1f2)
  • A bug in the polygon() rounding radius clamping formula that produced incorrect results for non-rectangular polygons has been fixed. (6ddda95)

Android

  • The tab strip fade effect was refactored to use compositor layers instead of PNG assets, allowing for variable-length fades and improved performance. (eeef6ab)
  • For WebView, HttpServerProperties (which caches support for protocols like QUIC) will now be persisted to disk, improving network performance across application restarts. (f14caa7)
  • The Window Controls Overlay feature is now enabled by default on SDK 35+, and its associated launch flags have been removed. (7da454a)
  • The Send-To-Self feature now marks entries as "opened" instead of deleting them, aligning Android's behavior with desktop platforms. (61812a9)
  • A fix was implemented to make tab reparenting on startup intent-aware, preventing hangs caused by stale global state. (2ad1705)

UI & UX

  • On Linux, a new client-side decorated frame view (FrameViewLinux) was added for non-browser widgets to provide consistent decorations on Wayland. (bec3336)
  • The caret positioning in the New Tab Page Realbox was refactored from JavaScript-based transforms to use CSS Anchor Positioning for improved robustness. (4dcb2fa)
  • The Bookmarks Tree View feature (#bookmarks-tree-view) has been enabled by default for all users. (3eacc12)
  • Accessibility has been improved by correctly exposing heading levels to Windows screen readers and enhancing Autofill popup announcements. (74f73f3) (dac9ec7)

Developer Tools

  • A new DevTools protocol command, Network.deleteDeviceBoundSession, was added to allow clearing of Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC). (feaf9e4)
  • The DevToolsManagerDelegate interface was refactored to introduce a new centralized hook, AllowInspectingTarget, for vetting DevTools attachments. A default implementation is provided to maintain backward compatibility. (bfdcab0)

2026-04-14

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-14

Overview

This daily summary covers 710 commits on the main branch, with 541 identified as relevant to developers. The period spans from commit (841492d) to (59d034a). Today's changes include an exceptionally high number of breaking API changes across Networking, UI, Graphics, and core services. Developers should pay close attention to the 'Breaking Changes' section to assess impact on their projects.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING A major refactoring of core networking Mojo interfaces removes deprecated [Native] types for SSLInfo, CertVerifyResult, HttpResponseHeaders, and others. This will break embedders using these network service interfaces directly. (c565345)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API chrome::FindTabbedBrowser() has been removed from browser_finder.h. Embedders must migrate to the new ProfileBrowserCollection class, which also returns a different type (BrowserWindowInterface*). (c7811f0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for ui::gl::HDRMetadataHelperWin has been removed. Code must be updated to use the new static Create() factory method. (10dba43)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API HelpBubble::AddOnCloseCallback() has been removed. It is replaced by two new methods: AddOnClosingCallback() and AddOnClosedCallback(). (e7b9096)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The NetworkContext::SetNetworkConditions API signature has changed to require a new throttling_client_id parameter, affecting DevTools and network simulation tooling. (c9c9dc6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The WebRTC RTCDataChannel.send() method will now throw a TypeError if a message exceeds maxMessageSize. This aligns with the spec but changes previous behavior where it would fail silently. (dfbbc3f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public C++ API content::WebContents::SetTabSwitchStartTime has a new signature to improve tab switch metrics. (424a1a6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the VIZ component, TextureDrawQuad::SetNew and SetAll no longer have a default parameter. Callers must now explicitly provide the is_tex_coords_normalized value. (c2fc4f3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ToolbarButtonProvider API has been refactored, removing GetAppMenuButton() and replacing it with GetAppMenuControl(). (0800caa)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Java methods isNightModeEnabled() and isForceDarkWebContentEnabled() have been removed from TabWebContentsDelegateAndroid. A new WebContentsThemeClient should be used instead. (7156569)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The SendTabToSelfModel::AddEntry method signature has changed, adding a new commit_confirmation callback. All implementations of this interface must be updated. (7ff606d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The return value contract for AutofillClient::GetProfile() has changed. It is now guaranteed to return a non-null Profile*, removing the need for null checks. (9b77d05)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public string constants for Perfetto data source names have been removed from the perfetto_service.mojom interface. They are now only available in the C++ header services/tracing/public/cpp/perfetto/perfetto_data_source_names.h. (61a9b97)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Shutdown() method has been removed from the PolicyInvalidator class. Cleanup is now handled via RAII. (2248ccf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On iOS, the public protocol SuccessfulReauthTimeAccessor has been removed as part of a simplification of the ReauthenticationModule. (18b43a7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The command-line switch kWebViewStartupTasksPlusMultiProcess has been removed as the associated feature is now enabled by default. (69cb556)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Java interface PaymentApp.InstrumentDetailsCallback has changed the signature of its onInstrumentDetailsError method to support more detailed error reporting. (3609d22)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public metrics-gathering functions have been removed, including ash::app_list::RecordZeroStateSuggestionOpenTypeHistogram and BookmarkStats::RecordBookmarkAllTabs. (8692e47) (3255c80)

Security and Privacy

  • To provide defense-in-depth against a compromised renderer, DevTools agent hosts for Service Workers and Shared Workers will now only bind their Mojo pipes when a DevTools session is actively attached. (4be40bb) (233b6b2)
  • The chrome.scripting.executeScript API now correctly enforces host permissions on all subframes, not just the top-level one, closing a potential IPC validation bypass. (e05c69e)
  • A recursion depth limit has been added to bookmark Pickle deserialization to prevent stack overflow from a maliciously crafted clipboard or drag-and-drop payload. (0e3f531)
  • The browser process now verifies that a Service Worker's main script fetch request matches the registered script URL, preventing a policy downgrade attack from a compromised renderer. (0f7954e)
  • A potential path traversal vulnerability in feedback_util::ZipString was fixed by validating the filename before use. (30d636a)
  • On Android, the system now ensures that memory file descriptors (memfds) are size-sealed before being handled, preventing Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) issues. (e5c6c0b)
  • Support for pre-M79 raw DPAPI ciphertext has been removed from os_crypt, eliminating a legacy fallback path. (db40741)
  • A bug in the cross-fade() CSS function that could leak EXIF orientation information from cross-origin images has been fixed. (1d5d289)

Web Platform and Rendering

  • The RTCDataChannel.send() method will now throw a TypeError if a message exceeds maxMessageSize, aligning with web standards. (dfbbc3f)
  • The CSS overflow: clip property will no longer show scrollbars on the non-scrollable axis, aligning its behavior with overflow: hidden. (2f04f82)
  • For WebGL, pending GL errors are now correctly preserved across a canvas resize, fixing a conformance issue. (73d0650)
  • The Navigation API now aligns with the spec to scroll to the top of the document when navigation.scroll() targets a non-existent fragment. (d6d5d3b)
  • A crash in TextIterator was fixed for cases where EmitsOriginalText() was enabled, which could cause out-of-bounds access with truncated text. (2dd1234)
  • The deprecated blink::WebString::FromUTF8() method was removed but then quickly reverted due to build failures. The API remains available. (64bea51) (0890a5c)
  • The FontSrcLocalMatching feature flag, enabled since M77, has been removed, making improved local font matching a permanent feature. (304f0cb)

Graphics and Media

  • The public constructor for ui::gl::HDRMetadataHelperWin has been removed and replaced with a static Create() factory method to remove a D3D11 dependency. (10dba43)
  • Hardware DRM playback failures on multi-GPU Windows systems have been addressed by ensuring the virtual video window is positioned on the correct monitor to select the right GPU adapter. (2992ee7)
  • A deadlock in WebAudio that could occur when stopping an AudioDestination with output buffering bypassed has been resolved. (dd97aa7)
  • In the VIZ component, TextureDrawQuad constructors now require callers to explicitly provide the is_tex_coords_normalized value. (c2fc4f3)
  • For ARC video encoding, a valid REC709 color space is now used by default. (092cbcc)

UI and UX

  • The HelpBubble API has been refactored for stability, replacing AddOnCloseCallback() with the more specific AddOnClosingCallback() and AddOnClosedCallback(). (e7b9096)
  • The chrome::FindTabbedBrowser() utility function has been removed. It is replaced by a new API on the ProfileBrowserCollection class. (c7811f0)
  • On Android, the BottomSheet can now act as a browser control, allowing it to be offset by the top-controls, by integrating with the BottomControlsStacker. (a2b879a)
  • On iOS, significant work was done on the App Bar and New Tab Page to fix layout issues in landscape and fullscreen modes. (68b3fae) (b3ca998) (8d832b4)
  • The multi-step PWA installation dialog has been refactored to include a new introductory view. (40c6553) (6623918)
  • On Android, the TabWebContentsDelegateAndroid API for themeing has been refactored, removing isNightModeEnabled() in favor of a new WebContentsThemeClient. (7156569)

Services

  • The AutofillClient::GetProfile() method contract has changed; it is now guaranteed to return a non-null Profile*, removing the need for null checks in client code. (9b77d05)
  • The SendTabToSelfModel::AddEntry method signature has changed to include a new commit_confirmation callback, breaking custom implementations of the model. (7ff606d)
  • The AutofillAiDefaultAvailability feature has been enabled by default on desktop, changing the opt-in flow for AI-powered Autofill features. (1f566c4)
  • For password sharing, a check was added to ensure the origin of a form's URL matches the signon realm's origin, preventing potential spoofing. (ff624fe)
  • The kMigrateAccountPrefs feature flag has been removed, finalizing the migration of account-scoped preferences on mobile platforms to align with desktop. (ac84d7d)
  • A use-after-free crash in FormFiller::UndoAutofill has been fixed. (c7c2ce4)

Networking and Performance

  • A major refactoring of core networking Mojo interfaces removes deprecated [Native] types, requiring significant updates for embedders using these interfaces. (c565345)
  • The NetworkContext::SetNetworkConditions API now requires a throttling_client_id to support multiple concurrent DevTools sessions applying throttling rules. (c9c9dc6)
  • A new caching mechanism was added to GURL::SchemeIsHTTPOrHTTPS() to improve performance on the navigation critical path. (ae2b997)
  • On Android, the UI update for loading state notifications has been moved out of the critical path of navigation, reducing blocking time. (9d95efd)
  • The AnchorElementPreloader was updated to select the correct Network Anonymization Key (NAK) based on the initiating frame, improving preconnect accuracy. (e3fbf66)

Build and Infrastructure

  • The effort to modularize the codebase continues, with sources for segmentation_platform, page_load_metrics, and media_galleries being moved into their own build targets. (80b4488) (0ec6be5) (5b56cb8)
  • cc::RefCountedBuffer now inherits from base::RefCountedThreadSafe instead of base::RefCounted to ensure atomic ref-counting and prevent concurrency issues. (5625e13)
  • New FYI CI builders for Linux Arm64 have been added to the build infrastructure. (e13b855) (1aab138)
  • The InProcessBrowserTestMixin was extracted into its own header to break dependency chains and improve build times. (9e207fd)

2026-04-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-13

Overview

On 2026-04-13, the Chromium main branch saw 613 commits, with 457 identified as relevant to developers. This day was marked by numerous breaking changes to core APIs, build configurations, and the removal of the Text Autosizer feature. The first major commit of the day was (129c0a6) and the last was (c2e9a49).

Core APIs & Services

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The ContentBrowserClient interface was updated. ShouldTreatURLSchemeAsFirstPartyWhenTopLevel(string_view) is replaced by ShouldTreatAsFirstPartyWhenTopLevel(const url::Origin&). Embedders overriding this method must update their implementation to use the url::Origin parameter. (9f52b02)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature for the virtual method net::CookieStore::SetCanonicalCookieAsync has changed. A default argument was removed, and callers must now explicitly provide the cookie_access_result parameter. Embedders who subclass net::CookieStore will need to update their implementations. (4d84d8a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: AccountSettingService has been refactored into a pure virtual interface. Consumers must update their code to depend on the AccountSettingService interface instead of the concrete AccountSettingServiceImpl class. (8a38787)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Core base/metrics APIs have been modified. Histogram constructors and PersistentCreate methods now require a name_hash parameter, breaking low-level integrations with the metrics system. (2edb1dc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public mojom interface PrintManagerHost::ShowScriptedPrintPreview() has been changed, removing the is_modifiable boolean parameter. Embedders implementing this interface must update their method signature. (d9e628f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The obsolete MitigationsEnabledFor3pcd function has been removed from CookieSettings, and the corresponding SetMitigationsEnabledFor3pcd method has been removed from the network::mojom::CookieManager Mojo interface. (1190624)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The platform-independent utility function base::subtle::YieldProcessor() has been removed from base/synchronization/lock_subtle.h via a revert. (cd356a8)
  • The VideoFrame::WrapSharedImage method overload that accepted a coded_size has been removed. This value can now be inferred directly from the shared image. (b14f02a) (04cfedf)
  • The chrome://policy/logs page has been migrated from the legacy WebUI messaging system to a modern, type-safe Mojo interface. (6a07b82)
  • The AIManager has been hardened with more explicit checks and granular error messages related to enterprise policies and user settings for on-device AI models. (9381fd2)

Build System & Infrastructure

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The //chrome/browser/net directory has been modularized. Source files were moved from the monolithic //chrome/browser:browser target into new :net and :impl targets, which will break downstream build configurations that directly reference these files. (888790c)
  • Enterprise-related source files have been moved from //chrome/browser into a dedicated //chrome/browser/enterprise build module to improve code organization. (8a22588)
  • The use_afl GN argument and AFL_DRIVER_DONT_DEFER environment variable have been removed to streamline fuzzer build configurations. (b691d02)
  • The deprecated TestBrowserClosedWaiter test utility has been removed and its callers migrated to the BrowserDestroyedObserver API. (dd0cca1)

Web Platform & Blink

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The Text Autosizer feature has been completely removed. The text_autosizing_enabled preference in WebPreferences and related logic in AwSettings are deleted. Embedders relying on this for text inflation will need to implement their own scaling logic. (4797e6b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The LayoutReinsertOnInFlowStateChange feature flag and its associated code have been removed. This includes the removal of several virtual methods from Blink layout classes, such as LayoutBlockFlow::ChildBecameNonInline, which may break downstream forks that override them. (f5d8e01)
  • The behavior of the stable Web API navigation.navigate() has changed. Calls with a javascript: URL will now reject with a NotSupportedError instead of being silently ignored, which could break sites relying on the previous behavior. (868ac71)
  • To align with the HTML spec, <meta> tags used for responsive iframes are now only considered valid if they appear within the document's <head>. (129c0a6)
  • A security issue where attr() values in CSS shorthands could bypass taint checks has been fixed. (0921752)
  • The SVG parser for <polygon> and <polyline> points has been refactored to parse numbers individually, improving alignment with spec-defined error handling. (5885775)

Android

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A refactor of PersistentStoreCleaner was reverted, changing its public Java API from instance-based methods back to static methods. Consumers of the recently introduced factory and instance methods will be broken. (66e6c2a)
  • Navigation logic was updated to support the SWITCH_TO_TAB disposition, allowing navigation to an existing tab with a matching URL instead of always opening a new one. (ca13bcf)
  • Syncing of site search engines (template URLs) is now enabled on Android, controlled by the kSyncSearchEnginesAndroid feature flag. (6c0a722)
  • The BottomSheetController now exposes a getPeekHeightPx() method, allowing for better integration with browser controls. (ceadac5)
  • The TabModelSelector interface now includes a getProfile() method to provide easier access to the associated profile. (8f196ec)

Security & Privacy

  • Cross-Origin Resource Policy (CORP) checks are now being enforced for the Service Worker Static Routing API's cache source. This change is gated by the kServiceWorkerStaticRouterCORPCheck feature flag. (e2b9187)
  • Client-Side Detection (CSD) for malicious sites will now trigger a check upon initial detection of a credit card form, in addition to the existing trigger on user interaction. This is controlled by the EnableDetectionTrigger feature parameter. (5543142)
  • On macOS, opening the applescript: scheme as an external protocol is now disallowed to enhance security. (71c38a1)
  • A path confusion vulnerability in the Payment Method Manifest crawler was fixed by using the post-redirect URL to resolve relative application paths. (aa2bdb2)

Developer Tools (CDP)

  • The Target.TargetInfo object for worker targets will now include a parentFrameId property. This allows DevTools clients to correctly attribute a worker to its owning frame in the target tree. (2fce35d)

Autofill & Passwords

  • The Autofill popup menu has been updated to support nested flyout menus, enabling richer UI for complex data types. (3bbb3c5)
  • The Mojo IPC for password generation requests has been updated to use FieldRendererId instead of indices, fixing a potential browser crash caused by out-of-sync frame data. (61c155e)

iOS

  • Implementations for Siri Shortcuts have been added, allowing users to open history, settings, passwords, and new incognito tabs via voice commands. (d921def) (99a1d6d) (9a3fea0) (279eb95)
  • Fullscreen support has been improved for iOS 17/18 by adjusting the WebView frame to correctly account for system UI changes. (d93e050)

Graphics & Rendering

  • A "flush-only" sync mechanism has been introduced for TreesInViz. This allows a renderer to notify Viz of resource deletions upon becoming invisible without triggering a full, unnecessary draw cycle. (5751c03)
  • BufferQueue usage is now enabled on Windows with partial delegation, which involves allocating a buffer queue to each drawn render pass to improve performance. (de96932)

2026-04-12

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-12

Overview

This digest covers 11 commits, analyzing changes in the Extensions API, UI components, and the Blink rendering engine. One major breaking change was identified in the Extensions API, requiring embedders to update their code.

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The virtual method ExtensionsBrowserClient::GetPrefServiceForContext() has been removed from the public API. Embedders who provide a custom implementation of ExtensionsBrowserClient will encounter a build failure. The new approach is to use user_prefs::UserPrefs::Get(context) to retrieve the PrefService for a given BrowserContext. (2a97ba7)

UI

  • Performance of vertical tabstrip animations has been improved through several optimizations, including sharing tab title implementations and avoiding unnecessary calculations (43868b098333bc27a343cea2f28897191c149b54). Support for a zoom bubble has been added to WebUI-based browsers, which appears when the user changes the zoom level (8768309c18f5012d8dfd51323385e5215334d485). A bug was fixed in the WebUI pinned toolbar to correctly hide actions that are marked as not pinnable (98242960c97c1f52313a8842893635fc6053e66b). (43868b0) (8768309) (9824296) (efcc15d)

Blink & Rendering

  • For privacy purposes, the default selection colors now use the CSS system colors Highlight and HighlightText instead of OS or theme-defined colors. This change prevents sites from using selection colors for fingerprinting (6751542bbb2bb4e5e65bd1ed249a9505875b7d40). A crash in Element::IsFocusableStyle was fixed, which occurred when checking for a <canvas> ancestor across frame boundaries (f983be2fa84b1d8aeb5e7714d0ce82db5711c1f8). The word segmenter was improved to correctly handle multi-codepoint emoji sequences, fixing bugs where certain emojis were broken apart (82279983c644a29625170d1be26e466589b77674). (6751542) (f983be2) (8227998) (baeae51)

Media

  • The JXL image decoder was made more robust by ensuring its frame scanner stops at the end-of-stream, preventing issues caused by malformed trailing bytes in an image file (f39e28fb9260c9e60b1ddef732f48aa786662354). (f39e28f)

Services

  • For the Facilitated Payments feature, new metrics have been added to log whether a payment-related iframe shares the same origin as the main frame (e3ac2ce1f452980edab69886e058acb10dc254c2). (e3ac2ce)

2026-04-11

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-11

Overview

This digest covers 32 commits, from refs/heads/main@{#(1613178)} to refs/heads/main@{#(1613332)}. A major breaking change was identified in the content public API, where the PrePrefetchService::Create method signature was altered. Other notable changes include security hardening for CORS requests, UI enhancements for Tab Groups, and various platform-specific bug fixes for iOS and ChromeOS.

Content API

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The factory method PrePrefetchService::Create has a new signature, adding three parameters: initial_origin_hint, initial_javascript_enabled_hint, and initial_should_append_variations_header_hint. This change, part of a refactor to pre-calculate headers on the UI thread, requires embedders who directly use this public API to update their calls to provide the new arguments. (508c306)

Networking & Security

  • As a security enhancement, no-cors requests using forbidden HTTP methods like TRACE and TRACK are now blocked. Previously, this restriction only applied to the CONNECT method. (d513cd2)
  • A global buffer overflow in Cast Remoting's protocol handling was fixed by adding bounds checking when casting proto enum values to their C++ counterparts. (0ff4ac1)
  • Potential use-after-free instances in the Remoting protocol's authenticator logic have been resolved. (174583c)
  • The EnterpriseRealTimeUrlCheckNewUrl feature for enterprise Safe Browsing has been enabled by default. (2590745)

UI & UX

  • As part of the "Glow Up" project, a new set of alternate colors for Tab Groups has been added behind a feature flag. The new colors are now reflected in the tab strip, group editor bubble, and WebUI tab search menu. (3efb98d) (a69fa3e) (87013f2)
