  • The Contextual Tasks feature now opens the side panel earlier during a Lens query to improve perceived responsiveness. Additionally, an "Undo" snackbar will now be shown when the bottom sheet is closed. (52fb6ea) (dfad241)

Blink & Rendering

  • A bug in Blink's GeometryMapper fast path has been fixed to correctly handle viewport mapping for out-of-process iframe (OOPIF) roots. This ensures remote transforms are applied correctly, preventing OOPIFs from returning incorrect local coordinates. The fix is gated by the FixVisualRectRemoteViewportTransform feature flag. (bb4aebf)

Internals & Memory

  • The base::AutoReset utility has been updated to use raw_ptr<T> instead of a raw C++ pointer, improving memory safety as part of the ongoing MiraclePtr migration. (c8d4183)
  • The mechanism for syncing accessibility preferences on ChromeOS has been updated to better handle conflicts between local OOBE settings and values from Sync. (5fc6fa7)

Platform Specific

  • Several iOS-specific build issues and crashes have been resolved, including a crash in extension processes related to PartitionAlloc, missing method implementations in ContentWebState, and a build error in WebNN CoreML. (9c0c4da) (eb775c6) (b3bb527)
  • On ChromeOS, the Fjord OOBE (Out-of-Box Experience) will now display an error screen if the selected device image fails to download. (ef90817)

Metrics & Telemetry

  • A new suite of unified metrics for voice search has been introduced to centralize logging logic, which was previously fragmented across different surfaces. (4ac984b)
  • An expired histogram, Extensions.ErrorCodeFromCrxOpen, which had no recording sites, has been removed. (b9bcdde)

2026-04-10

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-10

Overview

A total of 595 commits were made to the main branch, with 447 analyzed as relevant to developers. This summary covers the range from commit (52f8bb1) to (8f5b39c).

Core, Internals & Networking

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The base::task::SequenceManager API has been updated. SetDefaultTaskRunner is replaced by SetDefaultTaskQueue, and base::task::sequence_manager::ThreadControllerImpl with its factory is removed. Embedders must update task management initialization. (4b73a31) (36e7905)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The net::URLRequestJob interface has been updated. Public virtual methods like GetTotalReceivedBytes now return the type-safe base::ByteSize instead of int64_t, requiring subclasses to update their signatures. (72bf5dc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The optimization_guide::mojom::ModelBroker interface now uses string-based use_case identifiers instead of OnDeviceFeature enums for subscribing to and requesting on-device models. (dfe0d80)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public observer method AccessibilityAnnotatorBackend::Observer::OnContentAnnotationsAdded() signature has been changed to take a const ContentAnnotationsData&. (fd9d5b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The recently added public API content::CdmHelper and its header content/public/browser/cdm_helper.h have been removed via a revert. (7b96ee6)
  • The BrowserContext::UniqueId() method is being deprecated in favor of a new UniqueToken() method to provide a more robust identifier. (4f81ffc) (f2112b5)

Blink & Web APIs

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Support for width and height as presentation attributes on nested <svg> elements has been removed to align with web standards. This is a behavioral change that may alter the rendering of web content. (facfdaa)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The WebRTC interfaces RTCRtpScriptTransformer and RTC no longer inherit from EventTarget, altering their prototype chain and removing event listener functionality. (1bd6509) (115a4c6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The OverscrollEvent web API's overscrolling attribute is no longer nullable (changed from boolean? to boolean), which is a signature change affecting web content using this event. (e18c49c)
  • The WebCrypto API has been expanded to support the post-quantum key encapsulation mechanisms ML-KEM-768 and ML-KEM-1024. (b582009)
  • The experimental CSS random() function syntax has been updated to align with a new proposal. (f598bc9)

GPU & Media

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The lose_context_when_out_of_memory attribute has been removed from gpu_channel.mojom and associated factory methods like viz::ContextProviderCommandBuffer::CreateForRaster. GL state is now considered unrecoverable after an OOM event. (b7eadee)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public constructor for media::EncryptionPattern is now private and replaced by a static Create() factory method that returns an std::optional. This enforces validation on crypt_byte_block and skip_byte_block values. (006ccbd)
  • A workaround was added to disable hardware-accelerated RGB-to-YUV conversion on Linux systems with proprietary NVIDIA drivers to prevent camera feed issues on Wayland. (d2e6a6a)
  • Integer overflows in the H.264 parser discovered by ClusterFuzz were fixed by using checked arithmetic. (4986289)

Android

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The BottomSheetContent Java interface signature has changed, replacing canSuppressInAnyState() with canBeSuppressed(BottomSheetContent nextContent) to provide more granular control. (62ea9c7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy SMS infobar system for WebOTP has been removed. Public classes like WebOTPServiceInfoBar.java and headers like sms_infobar.h have been deleted as functionality moves to the modern message UI. (3872b1d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The SelectionDropdownMenuDelegate Java interface's show() method signature has been changed, replacing the HierarchicalMenuController parameter with a Runnable dismissMenuCallback. (c04fe64)
  • The SlimWebView is now used in the Contextual Tasks feature, allowing it to function on mobile where the full <webview> tag is unavailable. (5477c4f)

iOS

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The minimum required iOS deployment target has been raised to 18.0. Builds targeting older iOS versions will fail. (f870b78)

UI & Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The searchbox.mojom WebUI interface was refactored to a factory pattern. The setPage() method was removed, altering the initialization contract for SearchboxHandler and its subclasses. (a43ea83)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The AutofillSuggestionDelegate interface has new pure virtual methods (OnFilterChanged, OnSearchSubmitted), which will break any custom implementations of this delegate. (54c17d4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy ManagePasswordsIconViews UI component and its public header file manage_passwords_icon_view.h have been removed as part of a migration to a new page action icon. (822db52)
  • The unreachable GlicFreUI (First Run Experience) has been deleted from the codebase. (dc4535b)

Enterprise & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The FilesRequestHandlerBase::Delegate interface for enterprise content scanning has been significantly refactored. The signature of ReportWarningBypass was changed, and new pure virtual functions (CreateFileRequest, SetHandler) were introduced. (26c79c6)
  • A new enterprise policy, CpuPerformanceTierOverride, was implemented to allow administrators to override the performance tier returned by the CPU Performance API. (1bff823)
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in WebContentsImpl::ForSecurityDropFullscreen was fixed by using a WeakPtr to handle cases where a WebContents is destroyed during a nested message loop. (899e77f)
  • Subresource Integrity (SRI) checks have been hardened to require enforceable digest algorithms. (13b5274)

Build System & Code Health

  • The enable_surface_embed build flag has been removed, making the feature unconditionally available where WebContents is present. (b27035e)
  • On Android, FORTIFY_SOURCE=3 has been disabled as it was causing build breakages with the latest NDK. (a3fa49e)
  • Code modularization efforts continue, with components like push_messaging, nearby_sharing, and parts of extensions being moved out of monolithic //chrome/browser targets. (da19370) (37803b9) (63837c5)
  • A large number of expired histograms were removed from the codebase across many components to reduce technical debt. (eb0c859) (9cfa183) (f57f127)

2026-04-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-09

Overview

This daily summary covers 721 commits on the main branch, with 528 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from the first commit ((f3cfa32)) to the last ((1b28d58)). Today's updates include a large number of breaking API changes across core, content, media, and Blink, significant security enhancements, and continued refactoring efforts under the 'Bedrock' project.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public interfaces content::RenderFrameHostManager::Delegate and RenderWidgetHostOwnerDelegate have new pure virtual methods, requiring embedders to update their implementations. (b83f5c5) (e5f143f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core ContentBrowserClient interface was updated, changing the signature of RegisterNonNetworkWorkerMainResourceURLLoaderFactories. Embedders must update their implementations. (7d3c950)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core DOM method Element::setAttribute now requires an AtomicString instead of a String for its value parameter, breaking existing callers. (f9ec0ef)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING As part of the 'Bedrock' refactoring, the public functions chrome::FindBrowserWithID and chrome::FindBrowserWithWindow have been removed. Callers must now use GlobalBrowserCollection::GetInstance()->Find...(). (fb39bae) (2a4a86d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the media stack, media::VideoFrameFactory::CreateVideoFrame has a new signature, and the integer values of the media::Channels enum have changed, constituting an ABI/API break. (ed26c65) (898ad6b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public ocsp_result field was removed from net::SSLInfo, and the storage_access_api_status field was removed from the public mojo interface blink.mojom.NavigationParams. (f366471) (8f0e6af)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The DevTools Protocol command Network.emulateNetworkConditionsByRule was changed, deprecating the top-level offline boolean in favor of a per-rule property, requiring client updates. (6d608ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, WebView's certificate verification now correctly implements AIA backtracking. This significant behavioral change may affect sites that previously worked due to the incorrect validation logic. (1f87ad7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple other public APIs were removed or changed, including methods on base::Thread::Delegate, font_service.mojom, OmniboxAction, and the removal of an entire abandoned ChromeOS print preview feature. (e501d62) (bb555c3) (0087789) (15be144)

Security & Privacy

  • A same-origin check is now enforced for DedicatedWorker main script fetches, preventing asset exfiltration from Isolated Web Apps and Extensions. This was part of the ContentBrowserClient breaking change. (7d3c950)
  • A compromised renderer can no longer falsely claim audibility by spoofing a bypass of the browser's audio service. (c1f3553)
  • On Android, signature verification was added for the Entra SSO (Single Sign-On) provider to ensure only trusted Microsoft applications can supply authentication tokens. (9f8bf46)
  • A potential local privilege escalation vulnerability in Chrome Remote Desktop on Windows was mitigated by using user impersonation for file operations. (98ca974)
  • Opaque origins are now prevented from navigating to debug:// URLs. (bcb34fb)

Blink & Rendering

  • The element.scrollIntoView() web API now resolves its returned promise only after the smooth scrolling animation is complete, improving predictability for web developers. (cd669ef)
  • Support was added for CSS URL request modifiers (cross-origin(), integrity(), referrer-policy()) inside url() functions, initially for image fetches. (cd3f099)
  • The codebase is being updated to rename String::Substring() to String::DeprecatedSubstring() to discourage its use and encourage safer alternatives like subview(). (c6f8d3a)
  • Performance of CSS Grid Masonry layout was improved by deferring expensive calculations. (efcc63d)

Media & GPU

  • Performance was improved with the implementation of zero-copy VP9 alpha decoding, removing a memory copy. (fc79e8c)
  • The renderer can now request GPUInfo directly from the GPU process, a change that will facilitate faster GPU channel establishment in the future. (0677979)
  • Support for the long-obsolete Pepper3D feature was removed from the GLES2 command buffer decoder. (91c469d)

API & Feature Removals

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The unmaintained Expect-Staple feature was cleaned up, which included removing the public ocsp_result field from net::SSLInfo. (f366471)
  • The ChallengeURL prototype feature for WebAuthn was removed due to a lack of clear developer demand. (24b759e)
  • The kTrackingProtection3pcd and OptionMutationObserverImprovement feature flags were removed as their work is now complete and integrated into the standard behavior. (6cecf5c) (45eb9b7)
  • The unlaunched kAutofillEnableSupportForParsingWithSharedLabels feature was removed along with its associated code. (259e633)

Android & iOS

  • On Android, the full-screen exclusive access notification was changed from a Toast to a swipeable Snackbar for better user control and consistency. (0f6f492)
  • On iOS, full-page screenshots are now blocked when enterprise Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies are active. (3b2fea6)
  • The Android PDF viewer UI was modernized with updated toolbar and background colors. (0451006)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the WindowAndroid Java constructor was changed, renaming the trackOcclusion parameter to occlusionTrackingAllowed. (838401c)

Infrastructure & Refactoring

  • The ongoing effort to remove dependencies on the legacy Active Template Library (ATL) continued, migrating more COM objects to the modern Windows Runtime C++ Template Library (WRL). (c434495) (f3cfa32)
  • Initial infrastructure was added to support Chrome for Testing (CfT) configuration via a JSON file. (bca0df9)
  • Numerous modularization efforts continued to improve the build system, including componentizing chrome/browser/ui/test/ and moving headless mode initialization to a separate target. (e0cd9af) (ceaa354)

2026-04-08

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-08

Overview

This summary covers 793 commits to the Chromium main branch, with 622 deemed relevant for developers. The day was marked by an exceptionally high number of breaking changes across core browser, content, Android, and media APIs, requiring significant updates for many embedders. Notable changes also include the removal of the experimental Desktop Link Preview feature, security hardening in the media pipeline and password manager, and continued efforts in code modularization. The first commit was (fea075a) and the last was (1572e92).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core base::ObserverList now disallows re-entrant notifications by default on desktop platforms (Linux, Mac, Windows, ChromeOS). Code that relies on the old default of allowing re-entrancy may now hit DCHECK failures and needs to be updated. (83dc1d0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several core browser interface methods have been removed. The pure virtual function ZoomChangedForActiveTab() is removed from BrowserWindow, replaced by an observer pattern. The public functions chrome::GetTotalBrowserCount() and chrome::GetBrowserCount(Profile*) are also removed in favor of a new BrowserCollection API. Embedders must update to these new patterns. (0501de8) (ebcb57e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public virtual method ContentBrowserClient::IsIsolatedContextAllowedForUrl has been removed. Embedders overriding this method to enable isolated contexts for specific URLs will have their builds broken. (5697236)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new pure virtual method, HasSavedCompositorFrame(), has been added to the public content::RenderWidgetHostView interface. Embedders with custom implementations of this class must add an implementation for this new method. (1e42bd2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The JNI Zero class loading mechanism on Android has been overhauled. JniInit.java and JniUtil.java are removed, with functionality consolidated into JniZero.java. This is a fundamental change for Android embedders interacting with JNI. (cafc078)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signatures of CookieSettingsBase::ShouldIgnoreSameSiteRestrictions and net::CookieAccessDelegate::ShouldIgnoreSameSiteRestrictions have changed, adding a new const url::Origin& top_level_origin parameter. Subclasses must be updated. (1575beb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Significant public API surface related to Third-Party Cookie Deprecation (TPCD) metadata has been removed. This includes ContentSettingsType::TPCD_HEURISTICS_GRANTS, ContentSettingsType::TPCD_METADATA_GRANTS, and the entire components/tpcd/metadata directory. (708fe41)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public mojo API ContentSettingsManager::AllowStorageAccess has changed, removing the origin, site_for_cookies, and top_frame_origin parameters. Callers must update to remove these parameters. (8c04824)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public media::PaintCanvasVideoRenderer API has changed, removing GetRGBSharedImageCache() and GetYUVSharedImageCache(). This requires clients of blink::WebMediaPlayer to manage these caches themselves. (2a73df0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of public feature and field trial APIs, such as FeatureList::Accessor::GetParamsByFeatureName, has changed to use FieldTrialParams* instead of std::map<std::string, std::string>*. (9aa846e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public extensions::RuntimeAPIDelegate::OpenOptionsPage method is now asynchronous, taking a callback instead of returning a bool. Embedders with custom implementations must update their signature. (011e693)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method SetHTTPOriginToMatchReferrerPolicyIfNeeded has been removed from ResourceRequestHead in Blink. The browser process is now solely responsible for setting the Origin header. (64c60e5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core base/traits_bag.h utility has been refactored, changing how valid traits are defined. This will break embedder code that uses this pattern for configuring classes like base::TaskTraits. (0b91f88)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several exported OAuth scope constants (e.g., kChromeSyncSupervisedOAuth2Scope, kAssistantOAuth2Scope) have been removed from google_apis/gaia/gaia_constants.h, which will break embedders using them for authentication flows. (1751b75)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the SyncService method setInitialSyncFeatureSetupComplete has been changed to take no arguments, removing the source parameter. (481a77f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Android WebView Prerender API has been modified. The signature of setMaxPrerenders now takes a primitive int, and a new clearMaxPrerenders method has been added, breaking the previous convention of passing null to clear the setting. (18095d6)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The experimental CSSSelectorFragmentAnchor feature has been completely removed due to a lack of specification and security concerns. (d57f326)
  • The experimental Desktop Link Preview feature and its associated UI and feature flag (blink::features::kLinkPreview) have been completely removed from the codebase. (831a9eb)
  • A new CSS property, overscroll-behavior: chain, has been introduced. (89b1c90)
  • The outerHTML setter now correctly functions on elements parented by a DocumentFragment, aligning with the HTML specification. (a81c0b4)
  • The experimental html-in-canvas feature no longer supports nesting. Calls to drawElementImage or captureElementImage on a canvas that is a descendant of another canvas will now throw a NotSupportedError. (613498b)
  • A new WebTransportSendStream interface with sendGroup and sendOrder attributes has been introduced behind an experimental flag. (d6a3a21)

Security & Privacy

  • Multiple Use-After-Free vulnerabilities were fixed in components including PasswordFormManager, PermissionRequestHandler on Android, and WebSocketSpdyStreamAdapter. (c542194) (62cc950) (5d8f582)
  • The H.264 parser was hardened to validate first_mb_in_slice, preventing out-of-bounds data from being sent to hardware drivers. (94ad12b)
  • A permission escalation bug in the File System Access API was fixed, where revoking grants did not properly clear all associated state. (a4c6a71)
  • Shared Storage selectURL() query results are now partitioned by the context origin to prevent cross-origin information leakage. (9adcde8)
  • The accent-color CSS keyword is now restricted to the initial user profile when used in a Web App context to reduce cross-profile fingerprinting vectors. (0ff048e)

Code Health & Refactoring

  • A large-scale migration from the legacy base::Singleton pattern to the modern base::NoDestructor was performed across numerous Keyed Service Factories. (6d036b4)
  • The syncer::DataTypeSyncBridge API was simplified by providing a default implementation for CreateMetadataChangeList, removing redundant overrides in many subclasses. (22d418a)
  • The BluetoothDelegate interface now provides default implementations for its methods, reducing boilerplate code for embedders. (9ef8e29)
  • Continued modularization efforts have moved several components out of the monolithic //chrome/browser target, including device_reauth, browsing_data, and sync_file_system, improving build parallelism. (3df2ead) (3975e61) (35a64d9)

UI & Features

  • The "Send to your devices" context menu item is being replaced with a dynamic submenu that directly lists available target devices, improving usability. (1399633)
  • A confirmation dialog has been added for when a user attempts to disable Safe Browsing, reducing accidental deactivations. (420d2b0)
  • WebAuthn "Immediate Mode" has been enabled by default. (7b93b74)
  • A new entry point for the "Tabs From Other Devices" side panel has been added to the "History" submenu in the main 3-dot menu. (0964b4e)

2026-04-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-07

Overview

On April 7th, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 582 commits, with 502 being relevant to developers. This day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes across core APIs, including UI, networking, storage, and platform-specific interfaces. Key developments include the shipping of SharedWorkerExtendedLifetime, significant security hardening against Use-After-Free vulnerabilities, and an upgrade to TypeScript v6. The first and last relevant commits were (e1a3d4f) and (5a63e24) respectively.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The views::BubbleAnchor type, previously a std::variant, has been refactored into a dedicated class. This is a breaking change to the core ui/views public API affecting how bubble dialogs are created and anchored. (1ba6a69)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The iteration behavior of the Highlight web API has been changed from snapshot-based to live to align with the specification. Web content that modifies a Highlight set while iterating over it will now observe those mutations, which may break existing logic. (df5462c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In Blink's media pipeline, the MultiBufferDataSource::OnRedirect callback has been removed and replaced with SetTaintedCallback. This API signature change requires updates for embedders who customize media data loading. (96a511d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The StorageAccessHandle::Estimate() mojom interface method's signature has changed. It now returns an unsigned ByteSize instead of a signed ByteCount, requiring embedders to update their client implementations. (05ad8e7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On iOS, the public protocol CRWObscuredInsetsController has been renamed to CRWViewportController, which will cause compilation failures for embedders using the old name. (b5760c4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The return type for GetTotalReceivedBytes(), GetTotalSentBytes(), and GetReceivedBodyBytes() on the core net::HttpTransaction public interface has been changed from int64_t to base::ByteSize. (79bbb97)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method Database::ReportMemoryUsage has been removed from the sql/database.h header, breaking any embedder code that relied on it for memory diagnostics. (b93f6d1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On net::URLRequest, the bool allows_device_bound_sessions flag has been replaced with a DeviceBoundSessionMode enum, breaking consumers of this core networking API. (b057ca4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the JNI Zero layer was refactored, removing the public JniUtil.java class and consolidating its functionality. This breaks embedders who used the previous public Java classes for JNI initialization. (d75bfa0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several functions and the LocalPrintersObserver interface have been removed from the crosapi::mojom::LocalPrinter mojom file, breaking the public API contract for Crosapi consumers. (6d26714)

Web Platform, V8, & Blink

  • The SharedWorkerExtendedLifetime feature is now enabled by default, allowing shared workers to live longer under certain conditions. (070a08b)
  • A new beforefilter event has been implemented for customizable comboboxes and filterable selects, allowing websites to implement custom filtering logic. (5a63e24)
  • CSS letter-spacing behavior has been corrected for cursive scripts to properly apply spacing to word separators, aligning with the specification. (65df445)
  • To reduce layout shifts during navigation, HTMLFrameOwnerElement (iframes) will now retain the last known natural size of its content. (bbbabcf)
  • Parsing logic for the new hanging-punctuation CSS property has been implemented. (0e2f948)
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in WebRTC was fixed by ensuring RTCSessionDescriptionRequest callbacks are handled on the main thread, preventing heap corruption when an iframe with a peer connection is detached. (cdb9f6e)
  • The HTML-in-canvas feature has been prepared for an origin trial, with the addition of a UseCounter. (7bbf890)

UI & Frameworks

  • The ui::SimpleMenuModel now supports a secondary label in menu items, allowing for richer context menus. (a6fb10a)
  • Accessibility for ui::Views has been improved by correctly mapping kExpandedChanged events, generating kValueChanged events, and adding range value attributes to sliders, standardizing events between Views and web content. (cba5b0c) (330f20b) (bbd0adf) (3e04af2)
  • Startup performance has been improved by deferring tooltip computation for bookmarks on the bookmarks bar until they are actually needed. (4209a17)
  • In Autofill, accessibility annotator logic has been refactored from the UI layer (AutofillPopupControllerImpl) to the browser layer (AtMemoryController), clarifying the AutofillSuggestionDelegate interface. (5fe250d)

Security & Privacy

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in ServiceWorkerCacheStorageMatcher was fixed by moving main script response fetching to ServiceWorkerVersion, which has a more stable lifetime. (d17fe11)
  • Input validation has been hardened in the browser process to check TextureQuad texture coordinates and LayerContextImpl tile indices received from the renderer, preventing potential out-of-bounds memory access. (acafd83) (bac419a)
  • A path traversal vulnerability on Windows using "dot-dot-space" was fixed in ChildProcessSecurityPolicyImpl. (8f31946)
  • A potential Use-After-Free in SpdySession was fixed by re-validating stream iterators after they may have been invalidated by stream reset operations. (f97b03e)
  • On macOS, validation was added to IOSurfaceImageBackingFactory to check per-plane geometry, mitigating a potential out-of-bounds access from a compromised renderer. (3d4208c)

Platform Specific

  • On Android, Chrome Custom Tabs will now have a transparent background during page load for a smoother transition from the host app. (793c92b)
  • On iOS, a new DataProtectionTabHelper has been added to manage screenshot protection based on Enterprise Data Controls and Safe Browsing lookups. (ebf3f19)
  • On Linux, Chrome Remote Desktop now supports the updated CreateRemoteDisplay D-Bus interface in GNOME 50, while maintaining backward compatibility. (4c1aaa2)
  • On Android, support for the AltGr key on physical keyboards has been improved to correctly handle special character input in web content. (7e1d2b3)
  • On iOS, several first-run experience Lottie animations now use dynamic color providers, allowing them to adapt to light/dark mode without separate assets. (8e70ba8) (579a57d) (35f0256)

Build & Infrastructure

  • The TypeScript compiler has been upgraded to v6.0.2. Developers working on WebUI may need to update their code to address new deprecations (e.g., module -> namespace) and stricter type checking. (83a142e)
  • Various components, including ChromeLabsCoordinator, LensOverlayEntryPointController, and TabSearchToolbarButtonController, were migrated to use the UnownedUserData pattern, decoupling them from the monolithic BrowserWindowFeatures. (8b8f45b) (6d74b29) (a89d948)
  • The build system continues to be modularized, with components like //chrome/browser/feed and //chrome/browser/component_updater being moved to their own BUILD.gn files to improve dependency management. (bfec926) (879e523) (7d2f7e7) (c44b7b1)
  • A build issue with pkg_config.gni was fixed by adding include paths to cflags_c/cflags_cc instead of cflags, preserving the correct include order when using a sysroot. (ac83d92)

2026-04-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-06

Overview

This summary covers 282 commits on the main branch for April 6th, 2026, from (95b6d18) to (0c355e9). Highlights include several breaking changes to public C++, Mojo, and Rust APIs, the removal of the experimental ServiceWorkerAutoPreload feature, and the implementation of the omnibox.onInputEntered event for Android extensions.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method WillBeginImplCommit has been removed from the public cc::LayerTreeHostClient interface. Embedders implementing this interface must remove their overrides of this method to compile. (7010b87)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The CloseScanner method has been removed from the public crosapi.mojom.DocumentScan interface. Clients, such as Lacros, that use this service will break and must be updated. (feeba85)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the public blink.mojom.ServiceWorkerFetchHandlerBypassOption mojom interface, the kAutoPreload and kBypassOnlyIfServiceWorkerNotStarted enum values have been removed. This will break any client code referencing these values. (2bb2c50)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Significant breaking changes were made to the Mojo Rust public APIs. The raw_trap.rs module was removed from the mojo/public/rust/system crate, and the C-API was refactored, altering function signatures for accessing message data. (9f2d278) (a067235)

Rendering & Web Platform

  • The experimental html-in-canvas feature has evolved its API. The old drawElement and texElement2D methods are removed in favor of a new ElementImage interface and captureElementImage() method, which adds support for OffscreenCanvas in workers. (c184feb) (d189442)
  • The ServiceWorkerAutoPreload feature for subresources has been removed from the codebase, including its associated logic, tests, and mojom values. (2bb2c50)
  • A bug causing request stalling when a service worker fetch event is restarted has been fixed by properly resetting the preload state. (bc87e02)
  • In alignment with the HTML spec, workers created from data: URLs will now correctly have an opaque origin. This behavior is controlled by the kDataUrlWorkerOpaqueOrigin feature flag. (6067136)
  • The scroll and scale state of a page is now correctly preserved when restoring from the back-forward cache. (327c1fd)
  • To align with web standards, the initial value of the CSS rule-visibility-items property has been renamed from auto to normal. (d53e3d9)

UI & Features

  • The experimental "Detached Tabs" feature and its associated flag (kDetachedTabs) have been removed from the codebase. (c3a09d0)
  • Several improvements were made to Vertical Tabs, including adding metrics for the "Expand on Hover" behavior, enhancing the expand lock functionality, and fixing layout bugs. (8b102ad) (75d3b6b) (60f5e6f)
  • On the New Tab Page, animated doodles will now be disabled if the user has the "reduced motion" accessibility setting enabled. (af8a1d7)
  • The Autofill feature AutofillEnableAiBasedAmountExtraction has been enabled by default. (7eae6cb)

Android

  • Significant refactoring of multi-window tab launching logic has removed ChromeAsyncTabLauncher#launchTabInOtherWindow() and consolidated its functionality into the MultiInstanceOrchestrator API. (5394887)
  • The Adaptive Toolbar's translate button will now be correctly disabled when translation is blocked by enterprise policy. (20d1c71)
  • The back button gesture in-product help (IPH) has been updated to include a text bubble and a Lottie animation. (ded4bea)

ChromeOS

  • To prevent race conditions, the launch of ARCVM kiosk apps is now correctly delayed until after Beam ChromeOS D-Bus services are ready. (e5e0b19)
  • During shutdown, the HatsNotificationController is now destroyed earlier to ensure UMA metrics for surveys are properly recorded. (5ad968e)
  • The system routine controller now logs more detailed connectivity information to the session log for improved debugging of network-related issues. (f9df262)

Extensions

  • The chrome.omnibox.onInputEntered event is now implemented for Android, enabling extensions to provide site search and other actions from the omnibox. (6dc052b)
  • On Android, extension tab activation is now blocked in Custom Tabs (CCT) and PWA windows to prevent users from getting stuck on a tab without a visible tab strip. (b6afc10)
  • The EnterpriseExtensionDOMActivityTelemetry feature, which collects DOM activity for enterprise-installed extensions, has been enabled by default and is now tracked via a synthetic field trial. (1775f9a) (00f7844)
  • The enterprise.platformKeys, enterprise.platformKeysInternal, and enterprise.networkingAttributes extension APIs have been modernized by converting them from JSON schema to WebIDL. (f610734) (19c7e7f) (3440252)

Networking & Security

  • DNS resolution observability has been significantly improved by propagating detailed resolution information (e.g., source from cache, DoH, or system) into LoadTimingInternalInfo. (2e629dc) (3c5e3ba) (dd8d63c) (7288496)
  • A security vulnerability was addressed where system-triggered audio focus loss could incorrectly grant a frame a User Activation token. (55823cd)
  • A new feature flag, kIgnoreCorsPreflightPolicy, was added as a step toward deprecating kPreventPreflight and simplifying CORS handling for features like EventSource. (55b0c31)

Build & Infrastructure

  • A new testing utility, InteractionTestUtilSimulatorWebUI, has been introduced to allow automated interaction with tracked WebUI elements using the same APIs as for Views and WebContents. (3609c24)
  • The build system was improved by modularizing the //chrome/browser/download directory. (38b45dc)
  • The size of generated Mojo C++ files has been reduced for cases where no interfaces are defined. (4ca7c2c)

2026-04-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-05

Overview

This digest covers 3 commits from the Chromium repository. The analysis focuses on identifying major breaking changes for embedders, with this selection containing several targeted bug fixes across window management, payments, and iOS-specific UI. No major breaking changes were identified in this set of commits. The changes span from commit (599af56) to (ab2ec9c).

Bug Fixes

  • Fixed a Use-After-Free vulnerability in TransientWindowManager by replacing base::AutoReset with base::WeakAutoReset. This change prevents a crash that could occur if a window was destroyed during a synchronous callback, ensuring the manager's flags are not written to freed memory. (ab2ec9c)
  • Corrected an issue in the Facilitated Payments component where the PaymentLinkManager state could be improperly updated while an operation was already in progress. This ensures state consistency during payment flows. (2071ecb)
  • Resolved an iOS-specific bug that caused duplicate tabs to be created when a user approved a blocked popup via an infobar. The fix ensures the interaction handler correctly checks the infobar type before proceeding. (599af56)
Page 1 of 2
